mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing limits, prompting inflation fears and equity valuation compression that produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of late-cycle periods when INDPRO increases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 3.5 percent year-over-year force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 3.5 percent, or the threshold signal loses directional accuracy below 53%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment indicates a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer demand, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal renewed construction activity and durable goods demand, which directly improves corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance when HOUST increases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 baseline during a post-shock recovery phase indicates persistent supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings rebounds, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of post-shock periods when INDPRO remains below 100, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 54%.
